---
title: "Assignment 2"
author: "Emmanuella Ekpenyong (2696204), Fie Bergli (2902784) & Britta Abbink Spaink (2772949)"
date: "10 March 2026"
output: pdf_document
fontsize: 11pt
highlight: tango
---

### Exercise 1
#### a) 

```{r}
birth_weight <- read.csv("Birthweight.csv", header=TRUE)
```

We define a null hypothesis (H0): smoking and low birth weight are independent. We also define an alternative hypothesis (H1): smoking and low birth weight are associated. To test these hypothesis', we apply a contingency table, and a Fisher's Test. 
```{r}
table_smoke <- table(birth_weight$smoker, birth_weight$lowbwt)
```

```{r}
fisher.test(table_smoke)
```
The Fisher's test gives a p-value of 0.187. As our confidence level is a = 0.05, we see that 0.187 > 0.05. This result fails to reject the null hypothesis, which means there is no statistically significant relationship between maternal smoking anf low birth weight in this dataset.
